journal_entry.description: Borrowed $200,000 from bank at 6% annual interest for 3 years
journal_entry.rationale: Cash increases (asset) as the company receives loan proceeds. Notes Payable or Bank Loan Payable increases (liability) representing the obligation to repay the principal. The interest terms (6% annually, payable semi-annually) will be recorded as interest expense when incurred over the loan term, not at inception.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Cash received from bank loan increases cash (asset↑) and creates a long-term liability (liability↑). Interest will be accrued and recorded as incurred, not at loan origination.

output_credit_classifier.reason: Cash received from bank loan increases cash (asset↑) and creates a long-term liability (liability↑). Interest will be accrued and recorded as incurred, not at loan origination.
